boat.construction_details: Mahogany marine ply with additional oak ribs, copper riveted oak ribs, solid mahogany seat and transom,
boat.full_description:
Nutz, formerly Oboe, is a classic 12ft clinker built dinghy built in 2003 with marine ply with epoxy resin by a carpenter Richard Herbert in Essex. Number eight in a series of thirty. It conforms to a traditional clinker one design as found on the East Coast of Britain.

Makers specification  
"The dinghy is handcrafted in mahogany marine plywood, solid mahogany and oak. Construction is glued clinker planking with copper riveted oak ribs based upon traditional designs by Howard Chappelle.
Spars are clear fir, lines, sheets and halyards in white cotton-effect polyester and the dinghy is finished in clear yacht varnish to bring out the beauty of the wood.
The boat is part of a limited edition and each has a signed certificate."

It offers the choice of sailing with either one of two different rigs. A Gunter rig with the mast in a stayed position and a rising gaff with mainsail and jib up front. This is good for a crew and a more spirited sail.  

When stepped forward the mast requires no stays and a single halyard raises the alternative balanced Lug main. Ideal for solo sailing. Both rigs share the same boom and mast.     

Sails are by Jeckell of Wroxham.

Hull in marine ply laid in a lapstrake or clinker format, sealed with epoxy resin. Hull has additional  timber frames and copper roving.  

Seat and rear transom in solid mahogany.  Centreboard, tiller, rudder and oars in mixed solid wood and ply.

All fittings in Brass, including folding rowlocks.     

Marine quality buoyancy bags.

The boat was designed by the builder to be up to Class C inshore coastal waters standard.

Previously used at sea around Mersea Island, more recently inland lake use in Surrey.     

Canvass cubby on stainless frame for protection when rowing or motoring. Button on full canvas extension when mast is stowed.     

Combination piggy-back and road trailer to 750kgs, unbraked with EEC Type Approval
boat.name: Nutz
boat.short_description: Traditional clinker built sailing dinghy with the benefit of two rigs. Gunter, where mast is stayed and a balanced Lug where the mast is stepped forward and remains unstayed.
